rulings.Case Title: GLEN TINER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 4, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GLEN TINER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Ajudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 4, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Albay Construction Company's motion for summary judgment: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GLEN TINER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 4, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Moore Dry Dock Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the exposure issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Tiner's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff Glen Tiner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (pipe insulation) attributable to defendant. This secondary exposure occurred through plaintiff's father. Plaintiffs do not dispute that defendant is not liable for any alleged exposure to asbestos incurred as a result of Glen Tiner's direct exposure to asbestos while an employee of Moore Dry Dock. As to the duty issue, the declarations of Glen Tiner and Dr. Richard Cohen and the U.S. Maritime Commission document create a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Glen Tiner. Specifically, they create a triable issue whether defendant knew or reasonably should have known that the installation, removal, use, disturbance and/or cleaning up of asbestos-containing products or materials could create a safety hazard of secondary exposure for a member of an employee's household. Pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15, public policy dictates in favor of a creation of a duty in the present matter given that Francis Tiner was employed by defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Tiner's declaration, Mr. Ay's declaration and Dr. Cohen's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
